sanskrit:
यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तमहैतुकम्।
अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पं च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्॥
transliteration: yat tu kṛtsna-vad ekasmin kārye saktam ahaitukam | atattvārtha-vad alpaṃ ca tat tāmasam udāhṛtam ||
literal_meaning: That which clings to a single fragmentary task as though it were the whole, without reason, without truth-value, and meager—that is declared to be of the mode of darkness (tamas).
contextual_meaning: A mind caught in tamas mistakes a narrow, comfort-seeking routine for complete knowledge. It fixates on one repetitive action (overeating, doom-scrolling, substance use, compulsive gaming) as if it were the entire purpose of life, ignoring evidence, context, and long-term consequences. The resulting worldview is constricted, cynical, and emotionally flat.
psychological_state: Tamasik knowledge reflects a dissociative freeze response: attention collapses into a single sensory loop, time horizon shrinks to the next fix, and the prefrontal cortex is offline. The person is psychologically ‘asleep’ while appearing awake.
trigger_scenario: You come home exhausted, open a delivery app, and autopilot-eat junk while binge-watching until you fall asleep on the couch. Next morning you swear to stop, but by 7 p.m. the loop restarts; any broader perspective feels impossible.
anchor_text: Step beyond the rut; widen your view to let truth and possibility in.
